what_it_does: A read-only design document that sketches a reusable trigger registry and a Claude Code launch node, showing how board/file events flow through an event log, a trigger driver, and either cheap rule-routing actions or a supervised CC session launch, while classifying every proposed piece as REUSE, CLONE, or BUILD and flagging unresolved decisions for Tim.
observations: Proposes a trigger row shape in trigger/<id>.py with fields watch, when, action, output_target, scope, priority, and why
Reuses runtime/rules.py for both the when predicate (RULE_OPS) and cheap action routing (DESTINATION_KINDS)
Treats CC launch as a nodes/ dataflow module reached via run_node/run_graph, not as a new rule destination
States the driver is BUILT-NOT-ARMED, mirroring activation_driver's dormant, env-gated posture
Identifies a structured-output capture gap in the supervisor's done event and proposes a minimal additive fix
Highlights a self-trigger loop risk when a CC reply files a board item and re-emits board.filed
Tags evidence as Observed, Inferred, or Verified and every piece as REUSE, CLONE, or BUILD
Leaves multiple design decisions as explicit open questions rather than resolving them unilaterally
standouts: The floor reconciliation: CC launch is a node/graph run, not a rule verb, so FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS remains intact
The single biggest reuse win is runtime/rules.py for predicates and routing
The single biggest new piece is runtime/trigger_driver.py
The structured_output capture gap and proposed fix across _turn_done, msg_clone, and routine_runner
conventions: REUSE/CLONE/BUILD ledger classification for every proposed component
File-local, neutral tone with no ranking or winner declarations
Open questions explicitly flagged for a named decision-maker
MCP tool design follows the single parameterized tool with an op selector
concerns: Several load-bearing design decisions remain open (event taxonomy, create eligibility, scope shape, self-trigger guard)
Proposes edits to session_supervisor._turn_done without showing the exact surrounding patch context
Scope field references a forms design that is not present in this file
The always-on tick caller and arm gating are described as needs-Tim but not specified
notes: A careful, boundary-aware design that tries to launch Claude Code from triggers without adding a forbidden rule destination.
questions: Which 'forms design' is intended for the trigger scope field?
Why was mode_detection_rules kept off the create path despite being pure data?
Will the event-kind taxonomy become file-discovered, stay validated in-place, or be folded into activation contexts?
How will the always-on trigger-driver tick loop be gated and armed?
Which self-trigger guard will be chosen: structural responds_to exclusion or an origin marker on emits?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to design a reusable trigger registry plus CC-launch node, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It is trying to define a trigger system that can autonomously launch Claude Code sessions while staying inside the existing rule-routing and session-supervisor boundaries, reusing the predicate language and scheduler machinery already in the repo.
novelty: It separates rule-routing from CC-launch into two distinct dispatch surfaces, making the launch a graph node rather than a rule verb, and pairs that with a built-not-armed driver posture and a structured-output capture fix.
contribution: A detailed design sketch that defines the trigger row contract, classifies each proposed component by reuse/clone/build, identifies the structured-output capture gap, and articulates how autonomous CC launch can coexist with the existing rule floor.
summary_for_embedding: A read-only design document for a file-discovered trigger registry and a CC-launch node that routes board events through an event log and trigger driver to either cheap rule actions or a supervised Claude Code session, with reuse/clone/build classification and unresolved questions.
intention_for_embedding: To propose a floor-preserving, reuse-heavy trigger mechanism that can launch Claude Code without turning autonomous dispatch into a rule verb.
